OBJECTION HEARING CALENDAR
Off Calendar Jul-06-2022 Off Calendar

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-08-2022 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. DANIEL G. O'DONNELL of Doyle & O'Donnell (Tel: 916-706-2616) for plaintiff;
Atty. JAMES MOLINELLI, Esq. of Gilsleider, McMahon, Molinelli & Phan (Tel: 925-446-3118) for defendants Ciaran McCallum and Pualani Olivares.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANTS PUALANI OLIVARES AND MCCALLUM CIARAN'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT.

Matter is argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendants' motion to strike is denied. The factual allegations regarding defendant's driving while intoxicated and knowledge of the attendant risks are sufficient to support a claim for punitive damages. (See Taylor v. Superior Court (1979) 24 Cal.3d 890.) Paragraph 19 of the complaint alleges that defendant Olivares was aware that she was intoxicated, was aware of the dangers of driving while intoxicated, and chose to drive anyway. Such illegal and dangerous conduct is considered despicable and can support an award of exemplary damages.

Order signed.

Judge: Suzanne R. Bolanos; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/SRB)
